data.epi_content:
I returned.

For some reason, with a coffee cup in my hand.

The unique sensation I felt when my neck was severed came rushing back, making my whole body shudder violently.

"Ahh..."

Thanks to that, I spilled hot coffee on my snow-white thighs, slightly scalding my skin.

Rather than taking off my clothes and pouring cold water, I just let cool water drip from my fingertips in a gentle trickle.

"It stings."

So where am I now?

I entertain the thought that this might be heaven or hell disguised as my room.

But judging by the sensations I'm feeling, that doesn't seem to be the case.

Actually, this might be my room.

I mean, not this young lady's room, but the room where I originally lived.

Well, maybe I just had a long hallucination or collapsed from a heart attack and got up on my own.

With such absurd thoughts, I looked in the mirror.

And, of course, sitting there was just a cute young lady mocking the person having these meaningless thoughts.

Now, I can't even remember what I originally looked like.

When I think about what I want to drink, it's oolong tea.

Yes, iced oolong tea filled with ice from the café just a five-minute walk from home.

How much was it again?

Two thousand won, it was two thousand won.

What kind of person was I before?

Unemployed, not working at all.

Still, money wasn't an issue.

Like most people, my family and friends had disappeared.

In my case, I lost too much and ended up alone.

What did I want to do?

I gave up on human rights and became a graduate student, wanting to eventually become a professor.

My father wasn't a tenured professor but a research professor, but still, a professor is a professor.

But then the world turned upside down.

Not the world exactly, but society.

I went north wearing green clothes, and lost my arms and legs.

But who would hire someone with missing limbs?

Not only students, but even professors would look me over with annoyed glances.

Unable to properly network, I'd probably just end up spinning my wheels as a part-time lecturer until the end, so I gave up early.

"But now, why does any of that matter?"

I struggled so hard to live that I even killed people.

Is this my punishment?

No, communists aren't people, so I never actually committed murder.

Suddenly my mind cleared.

I walk to the drawer and open it.

Inside were a few gold coins, scattered about in disarray.

I pick up two gold coins and head to the store that sells luxury goods within the Academy.

Whatever time it is now, whatever happens next, whatever happened between Vivian and me—what does it matter?

I'm going to smoke.

I saw the Crown Prince stuffing tobacco leaves—maybe cannabis leaves—into his pipe and smoking it, so there must be something available.

I need something to put in my mouth and smoke or I feel like I'll lose my mind.

So I put on a fresh uniform, put on my shoes, and walk out of the dormitory.

Looking carefully, I could see a bespectacled gentleman striding toward this place from far away.

He might be hesitating whether to come in or not.

Anyway, it was obviously Evan, so I quickly moved away to avoid wasting energy meeting him and forgetting to buy tobacco.
data.epi_content2:
Past the restaurant street, I head toward the street selling all sorts of small trinkets.

There are quite a few—no, an enormous number of people.

Perhaps because it's evening.

Anyway, I enter a store selling luxury items like coffee, tea, tobacco, and alcohol.

Perhaps because they carry high-end products, it wasn't crowded inside.

When I reached for a cigar in a wooden box, my hand touched someone else's.

"Well, if it isn't the young lady of House Mecklenburg."

It was the Crown Prince.

I bowed slightly at the waist, not so much out of respect but just inclining my head slightly.

"Since we're in the Academy, I won't stand on ceremony, Your Highness."

"It doesn't matter. But speaking of manners, how about showing some by letting me have the last box?

After all, a woman smoking isn't exactly considered a positive activity."

"Who would criticize someone seeking comfort by sending a small wisp of smoke skyward, hidden in her room where no one can see?"

"When you put it that way. I'll make a special exception for you.

I suppose I'll have to sit with my pipe again, puffing away tediously."

Saying that, he releases his grip on the box we were both holding.

I bow my head once and complete my purchase, then walk back toward my room.

But then I remember that Evan might be heading to my room, and my steps falter.

So I walk to a suitable bench next to a small pond.

It's a place where I often came to chat with Evan and Vivian.

There, intending to blow my thoughts away with smoke, I create a small flame at my fingertip and light the cigar, putting it between my lips.

And when I inhale the smoke once...

"Cough!! Hack! Cough! Ughhhh..."

I realized this young lady's body was smoking for the first time.

From my throat to my entire face feels nauseated, creating a feeling both familiar and unfamiliar, giving me another sense of disconnect.

Of course, in my mind I was once a heavy smoker so inhaling smoke feels familiar, but this young lady's body is struggling terribly with it.

"I saw you pick that and thought you were a heavy smoker, but I guess I was wrong to give it up."

"Following a woman around isn't exactly a virtuous trait, Your Highness."

"...Perhaps."

I finally gave up. There's no way I can smoke this.

It feels like my lungs are particularly weak, like there are thorns pricking me every time I breathe in.

After repeatedly inhaling and exhaling the smoke, I realized I couldn't possibly smoke it, so I just extinguished it and put it down.

It seems there's nothing I can do with this young lady's body.

Even when I want to indulge in a luxury item, I can't properly control my body.

Fortunately, there was someone nearby who could take care of the item.

"One has already been lit, but the rest are brand new from the store.

Would you like them as a gift?"

"I've been waiting for you to say that."

I hand the wooden box full of cigars to the Crown Prince.

He smiles with just the corners of his eyes and snatches the box as if afraid someone might take it away, then perversely opens the lid and smells it.

Then he sits down next to me, about an arm's length away, crossing his legs.

He opens the wooden box he just received from me, mutters something to the air, and a wavering flame appears, spinning around and attaching itself to the end of the cigar.

From that sight, I could tell that at least the Crown Prince wasn't using magic directly himself.

"Have you heard the news?"

As if he wasn't really expecting an answer, he inhales the smoke once, exhales, and continues speaking.
data.epi_content3:
"Your family is ruined, not yet, but it will be.

Whether it's debt or rebellion, somehow it will happen."

I didn't answer this either.

I just stared for a long time at the small, nameless fish swimming around in the pond.

The fish was swimming in circles in the round pond, repeatedly going over the same areas, picking up food.

After swimming around for a while, it hesitates slightly when it bumps into the wall.

Being a fish, it quickly forgets and repeats the same behavior.

"I know."

"Usually, when someone is alone with a person of higher status who could easily help their family, they try to gain something. Were you abused by your family?"

What abuse?

I was raised with love.

To see everything in the world positively and to be able to give love to others.

But those few lines ruined everything for me.

I'm just an unimportant mechanism written on text, meant to make Vivian sad.

So if I break, throw me away.

Just change me. Please.

"You have no reason to help me, and no will to do so."

He nods.

"That's right, I don't.

If you were asking for help, maybe, but you don't seem to be.

So I'll share one cruel truth with you."

When he opens his mouth, the scent of cedar rises heavily.

Probably, the tobacco was thoroughly coated with fragrance.

"As you know, life will be a bit difficult for you from now on.

Even after graduation, without backing, what could change?

If you're going to cause trouble, handle it quietly in your room.

Your calm type is the most dangerous."

"I'll do that this time."

The Crown Prince twitches his eyebrow slightly.

Did he hear something that displeased him?

"Sometimes I see people who cross the line. When that happens, come find me.

I can offer at least a tiny bit of help."

As if I could ever find you.

You'll always be surrounded by people.

"I'll be going now.

I don't like this place—too many bugs, and I hate the smell of water."

He dusts off his backside once and walks away somewhere.

I also sit on the bench staring at the fish for a long time, then get up and walk back to my dormitory room.

Rather than feeling like my head was going to explode from not being able to smoke, the feeling of going crazy from the smoke was stronger.

The smell of tobacco was more intense than the smell of water.

As I was walking, I just crouched down, bringing my knees together, and cried a little.

"I guess I don't want to die."

Even the emptiness of my repetitive life, I wanted to feel it again.

That was the thought I had when my neck was cut off and my face was flying through the air.

But I cried helplessly because it seemed the world didn't want to let me live.

So, no, just. Just that.

Wanting to live, without a reason. That kind of thing.
